description: Recovery-code format invariants. Codes are 12 ASCII characters in three hyphen-separated groups of four. The alphabet excludes 0/O/1/I/L (ambiguous when read aloud). Generation is non-deterministic, so this fixture exercises the format predicate `is_valid_recovery_code` rather than fixed string outputs.

tests.description: A canonical 12-char three-group code in the v0.2 alphabet MUST match the format predicate.
Digits-only codes (excluding 0 and 1) MUST be valid.
Mixed letters and permitted digits MUST be valid.
Codes containing 0 MUST fail format validation (ambiguous with O).
Codes containing O MUST fail format validation (ambiguous with 0).
Codes containing 1 MUST fail format validation (ambiguous with I/L).
Codes containing I MUST fail format validation.
Codes containing L MUST fail format validation.
Lowercase codes MUST fail format validation. Implementations normalize at the verification boundary; the canonical form is uppercase.
Codes with fewer than three groups MUST fail.
Codes without hyphens MUST fail format validation. Normalization MAY accept them at the verification boundary, but the canonical form requires hyphens.
Groups that aren't exactly four chars each MUST fail.
